findings.evidence: `rg` confirms only `squareClass_of_sq_mem`/`squareClass_of_sqrt_mem` at lines 131 and 237; roadmap `Multiquadratic/Targets.lean` lines 42-45 states the downstream degree target over `{ι : Type*} [Fintype ι]` and `IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range r)`.
findings.fix: Add a public `Fin n`/finite-index wrapper, using `sqrtTower_eq_adjoin_fin_range`, whose conclusion returns a `Finset (Fin n)` (and preferably an arbitrary `[Fintype ι]` wrapper) so the result feeds the roadmap `hindep : ∀ S : Finset ι, ...` without reindexing boilerplate.
findings.issue: `squareClass_of_sq_mem` exposes only the internal `ℕ`-indexed tower form and returns `T : Finset ℕ` plus `↑T ⊆ Set.Iio n`, while the roadmap target is stated for `IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range r)` over an arbitrary finite index type.

findings.evidence: `rg` shows `exists_add_mul_of_mem_sup_adjoin_sq` at line 33 and `mem_sup_adjoin_sq` at line 102 have the same conclusion, while `mem_sup_adjoin_sq_iff` at line 110 is the usable membership restatement; roadmap `README.md` lines 21-22 and 88-91 names `mem_sup_adjoin_sq` as part of the migrated engine.
findings.fix: Keep one elimination lemma and make the roadmap-facing `mem_sup_adjoin_sq` name be the iff characteristic lemma (or remove the duplicate alias and expose only `mem_sup_adjoin_sq_iff`, with the intended simp/grind attribute if desired).
findings.issue: The public quadratic normal-form API exports two identical elimination lemmas, `exists_add_mul_of_mem_sup_adjoin_sq` and `mem_sup_adjoin_sq`, while the actual membership characterization is separately named `mem_sup_adjoin_sq_iff`; this makes the advertised `mem_sup_adjoin_sq` name misleading and bloats the public surface.

summary: The tower API gaps from the prior review are fixed, but the exported descent/normal-form surface is still not shaped for the stated finite-index Layer-0 target. Add the finite-index characteristic descent wrappers and clean up the duplicated quadratic membership API before downstream degree proofs depend on it.
